title: Jolli
content:

{{Quote|Jolli?! What's gotten into that blood-thirsty little spitfire now?|Crimson Jack|<ref name="marvel12">[[Star Wars (1977) 12|''Star Wars'' (1977) 12]]</ref>}}
'''Jolli''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] who served on the crew of the notorious [[Crimson Jack/Legends|Crimson Jack's]] [[Crimson Jack's crew/Legends|gang]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Short-tempered and quick-triggered, Jolli was abandoned by her fugitive father as a young girl, instilling in her a deep hatred of men. Driven to prove herself as better than any man, Jolli became adept with a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] and a skilled [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] before joining Crimson Jack's crew. Jolli soon became one of Jack's most trusted crew members, but her deep-seated insecurities from her childhood led to a breakdown in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]].

That [[Standard year/Legends|year]], Jack captured [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], a [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] to whom Jolli developed an attraction. As Solo guided Jack to a supposed Rebel treasury on [[Drexel/Legends|Drexel]], Jolli was unable to reconcile her hatred of men with her attraction to Solo, leading her to display violent and erratic behavior. When Solo escaped, Jolli was picked to lead Jack's starfighter complement in a battle against Solo's [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], but saw her [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wing]] irreparably damaged in the sortie. Infuriated by Jack's refusal to tow her back to his [[Crimson Jack's battlecruiser|cruiser]], Jolli rammed the remains of her Y-wing into the cruiser's munitions deck, [[Death/Legends|killing]] herself but utterly destroying Jack's pirate operation.

==Biography==
===Adventures with Crimson Jack===
{{Quote|Ah, Jolli! When will you learn that a man can always be killed later... but a lost opportunity is very difficult to revive.|Crimson Jack to Jolli|<ref name="marvel11">[[Star Wars (1977) 11|''Star Wars'' (1977) 11]]</ref>}}
[[File:JollisUnjollyParents-MSW15.png|thumb|left|150px|Jolli and her mother are abandoned on a frontier world.]]
A [[Human/Legends|Human]], Jolli grew up on the run, the daughter of a fugitive from the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. For a time, Jolli and her parents lived in an outlaw hideout on a frontier [[Planet/Legends|world]], but when she was still a young girl the Empire tracked her father down and attacked the stronghold. In the ensuing firefight, Jolli's father decided to leave Jolli and her mother behind, deciding that the two of them would be dead weight as he continued his flight from justice. Left behind in the burning wreckage of their home, Jolli was orphaned shortly after when an Imperial aerial [[Torpedo/Legends|torpedo]] killed her mother. Alone on the fringes of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], Jolli grew into a short-tempered, man-hating hired gun with considerable skill with a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]]. Eventually, Jolli took up [[Pirate/Legends|piracy]], joining the notorious [[Crimson Jack's crew/Legends|gang]] led by a man known as [[Crimson Jack/Legends|Crimson Jack]]. Jolli subsequently took up residence on [[Crimson Jack's battlecruiser]], a former Imperial [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] acquired by Jack after it was damaged in a battle with [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]].<ref name="marvel15" />

Although she was still relatively new to the piracy game by [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], Jolli quickly became one of Crimson Jack's most trusted guns, honing her craft as Jack's gang plundered unfortunate [[Starship/Legends|ships]] across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. That [[Standard year/Legends|year]], Jack's [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] ensnared a particularly notable ship: the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], personal [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] of [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], a notorious [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]], Rebel hero and an acquaintance of Jack's. Jolli accompanied Jack as part of the boarding party, pulling a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] on Solo and his [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] companion [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] when they considered making a stand. Although Solo nearly needled Jolli into starting a firefight, Jack calmed her down and together they raided the ship, leaving the ''Falcon'' with an amount of treasure that shocked Jolli—<ref name="marvel7" /> but not before leaving a [[Homing beacon/Legends|homing beacon]] on the vessel.<ref name="marvel11" />

[[File:Jolli2.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Jolli with Leia Organa.]]
Jack's gang soon found another big catch: [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], former [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] and a rebel leader, whom they captured traveling alone across the starlanes. Although they hoped to receive a large ransom from the Rebels for Organa's safety, the Princess proved to be feistier than expected. Instead, Jolli and the gang tracked down the ''Falcon'', coming upon it as it departed the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Aduba-3/Legends|Aduba-3]] and once again taking hold of the vessel. However, this time they didn't plan to rob Solo and Chewbacca. Instead, they planned to kill the smugglers, slowly and painfully, as an example to Organa of what could happen to her if she didn't cooperate. Setting their blasters to slow burn, Jolli and several gunmen fired at their captives, only to be overpowered by the hulking Wookiee Chewbacca, who simply moved through the blasts and incapacitated several of Jolli's men.<ref name="marvel11" />

In a standoff, Solo and Crimson Jack decided to negotiate, with Solo promising that he could help them gain an even greater treasure than the gang had previously taken from him. Jolli, Jack, Solo, Organa and Chewbacca returned to Jack's private quarters for a feast, where the conversation became more amiable, although Jolli kept her blaster trained on Solo throughout. Solo revealed that he and Organa were acquainted: Solo had been previously rewarded by the Rebel Alliance for saving the Princess from the Empire's clutches and the two had developed a romance, which Organa seemingly corroborated before kneeling and hitting Solo to the floor. She then bluffed by claiming that the Rebel treasury was in the [[Drexel system/Legends|Drexel system]]. Jolli led Organa back to her cell in the ship's brig. Although Jolli expressed some admiration for how Organa handled the situation, her respect turned into an explosion of anger when Organa complimented Solo's kissing ability, which triggered Jolli's hatred of men.<ref name="marvel11" />

===Breakdown and death===
{{Quote|That Han Solo stirred up some strange feelings inside me while he and Princess Leia were our prisoners—but now's the moment when I show the galaxy I can write the end to any man!|Jolli|<ref name="marvel15" />}}
Organa's comment on kissing proved to have a lasting effect on Jolli, awakening an interest in Solo that both obsessed her and repulsed her, and sent her into rages. After the battleship approached the Drexel system, Jolli made a comment about kissing while in the ship's [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]], prompting several crewmen to make advances on her—Jolli responded by letting loose with her blaster, nearly killing them all in the process. Crimson Jack arrived in time to slap the weapon out of her hand, but agitated by Solo's presence, Jolli dealt the [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] smuggler a firm slap. Despite the violent outburst, Jolli soon resumed her place by Jack's side when the ship entered range of [[Drexel/Legends|Drexel]]. However, upon arrival, the ship's scanners indicated that Drexel was a [[Waterworld/Legends|waterworld]] which would make it an impracticl base for the Rebel Alliamce. The crew sensed something was amiss. Jack believed that the Rebels couldn't possibly have a treasury on such a world, and fearing that Solo was attempting to pull a fast one, Jolli and her captain drew their blasters.<ref name="marvel12" />

[[File:JolliGoesBoom.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Jolli rams her Y-wing into the battlecruiser's munitions deck.]]
Staring down a pair of gun barrels, Solo asked for Organa to be brought up from the ship's brig, promising that she would be able to help explain everything. Jolli was dispatched to bring Organa up to the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]], although it became a tense trip after Organa irritated Jolli by suggesting that her animosity towards Solo might be because she was attracted to him. However, shortly after Jolli and Organa reached the bridge, the entire cruiser was rocked by some sort of gravity disturbance: a [[sonic jammer]] operated by [[Ship-dweller|ship wreckers]] on Drexel's surface had ensnared the craft and was trying to pull it in.<ref name="marvel13">[[Star Wars (1977) 13|''Star Wars'' (1977) 13]]</ref> Her thoughts still swirling around her attraction for Han Solo, Jolli was caught unawares by Organa, who managed to slip away with Solo and Chewbacca and reach the ''Millennium Falcon'', fleeing to the planet below.<ref name="marvel14">[[Star Wars (1977) 14|''Star Wars'' (1977) 14]]</ref>

Matters were far from settled, however. Although the crew of the ''Millennium Falcon'' managed to disable the sonic jammer keeping them all bound to Drexel, Crimson Jack's gang remained in orbit, waiting for Solo and his friends to poke out their heads.<ref name="marvel14" /> Anticipating a fight, Jack set Jolli on overseeing repairs on his [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] complement, most of which had been damaged during Solo's escape. Under Jolli's direction, three of the craft were restored to function, which Jack proclaimed was good enough for him. With a showdown with Solo coming, Jack let Jolli know that he intended to use her bloodthirsty streak to full effect. Placed behind the controls of a [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wing fighter]] and leading the three-person [[Raider Squadron]], Jolli was sent down to Drexel's surface, firing upon the ''Millennium Falcon'' and chasing it back up into the crosshairs of the two other fighters in space. However, although they attempted to drive the ''Falcon'' into the cruiser's tractor beam, Solo skillfully evaded the onrushing fighters and the ''Falcon''{{'s}} guns killed Jolli's two squadmates.<ref name="marvel15" />

Alone and with the ''Falcon'' bearing down on her, Jolli responded by roaring right back at Solo's freighter, attempting to clear the ship without exposing herself to the ''Falcon''{{'s}} [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]]. She very nearly succeeded, scraping her Y-wing across the ship's [[Hull/Legends|hull]] and seriously damaging both the ''Falcon'' and her own fighter. The impact sent Jolli's craft out of control, and as the Y-wing started to break apart, Jolli radioed in a request for Crimson Jack to send a magnetic pulse beam to bring her back to the ship. However, Jack refused, fearing that it would interfere with his signal system, leaving Jolli on her own. Infuriated, Jolli resolved that if she was going down, she was bringing Jack with her. As Jack met Solo to parlay out in space, Jolli turned the listing remains of her Y-wing towards Jack's battlecruiser, cannons blazing. As her cannons took out Jack's backups outside the ship, Jolli made a suicide run on the cruiser—as she slammed into the ship's munitions deck, her Y-wing's remaining live torpedos caused an explosion that utterly destroyed the cruiser. Shortly afterward, Solo shot and killed Jack, the only survivor of the blast. While searching the wreckage, Solo and Organa found Jolli's corpse in the mangled remains of her Y-wing. Before blasting away from Drexel in the ''Millennium Falcon'', Solo gave Jolli the kiss that she had once obsessed over.<ref name="marvel15" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|I'd put a blaster in his belly if he tried kissing me!|Jolli, after meeting Han Solo|<ref name="marvel11" />}}
[[File:CrimsonJolli-MSW14.png|thumb|right|200px|Jolli and Crimson Jack.]]
Adept with a blaster<ref name="marvel11" /> and a talented starfighter [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]],<ref name="marvel15" /> Jolli was exceptionally quick-triggered and swift to anger, ready to start shooting at even the slightest provocation. Oftentimes, the only person who would be able to calm her down was her boss, Crimson Jack. Jack took it as a sign of Jolli's relative inexperience in the piracy game:<ref name="marvel7" /> he preferred that she keep her gun in the holster, as it was difficult to get more [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] out of dead men. Jolli's anger sometimes surprised and disturbed her with how suddenly it erupted, although one consistent trigger was a deep-seated hatred of men that she carried with her throughout most of her life.<ref name="marvel11" /> Jolli was driven by the idea of being tougher and harder than any man around her, fueling her hostility towards the male sex. This stemmed from her father's abandonment of her as a child, as her father's comment that she and her mother weren't "good enough" drove her to constantly feel the need to prove to everyone around her that she was, in fact, good enough. That insecurity had an effect on the people working under her: when serving as a flight commander, she constantly felt the need to assert that she was in charge, irritating her fellow pilots.<ref name="marvel15" />

However, after meeting Han Solo, Jolli developed an attraction to the smuggler, which interacted with her loathing of men to create a dangerous combination. Jolli began obsessed with the idea of kissing Solo after a comment from Leia Organa—she soon began talking about it unbidden with crewmates on the ship, and exploding with rage when they responded with advances.<ref name="marvel12" /> Jolli's attraction to Solo was obvious to the people around her, but the pirate only responded with angry denial when it was pointed out.<ref name="marvel13" /> Although Jolli seethed with barely restrained hostility towards most people she interacted with, she had some respect for Organa, admiring her ability to handle Solo.<ref name="marvel11" /> However, she was jealous of Organa's presence on board, lashing out by flirting with Jack's crewmen and then spurning them.<ref name="cswe">{{CSWECite|II|171|Jolli}}</ref> The pirate was known for wearing skimpy clothes, but her sartorial choices were expressly not to please men: rather, they had a combat application, giving her maximum freedom of motion.<ref name="ff134">{{FactFile|134|GAR 17-18|Garments and Couture}}</ref> Jolli had [[Color/Legends|purple]] hair and green [[Eye/Legends|eyes]].<ref name="marvel7" />

==Behind the scenes==
Jolli first appeared in [[Star Wars (1977) 7|''Star Wars'' (1977) 7]], written by [[Roy Thomas]] and released in [[1977]].<ref name="marvel7" /> Jolli later returned to become a major supporting character in a story arc beginning with [[Star Wars (1977) 11|''Star Wars'' (1977) 11]],<ref name="marvel11" /> before being killed in [[Star Wars (1977) 15|''Star Wars'' (1977) 15]].<ref name="marvel15" /> During her appearances in the [[Star Wars (1977)|Marvel ''Star Wars'']] series, Jolli was illustrated by [[Howard Chaykin]]<ref name="marvel7" /> and [[Carmine Infantino]].<ref name="marvel15" />
